9:41 I agree with the 5 minite, but I think one should use 1 main timeframes & 2 other main timeframes. One higher and one lower. Lower, because it can help give you a good warning if something is accepting or rejecting VWAP/bouncing etc. Higher, because you can see patterns a little cleaner sometimes. And have at least 1 higher in order to see where higher trend/support+resistance levels can be. Eg. You can be trading a smaller part of a much larger structure and you should really know what that structure is forming

Your system is highly interpretive and thus is not reliable in real time. The human mind will be scrambled finding a buy or sell signal. You give a lot of if than else items. You need to program all these items into a simple green up arrow and a simple red down arrow, then a person's mind won't get distracted .

I find using vwap helpful but precautions should be taken. If trading using 30 mins or hours timescales, the daily session is not helpful. I would suggest using the weekly vwap. Ideally a three day or two days vwap would be better. But trading view does not allow for that.

this is basically how I trade. I just use different bands and buy/sell strategies. But the VWAP logic works. Just be mindful about support levels (not to set buy/sell price on VWAP line itself, support levels are better) and previous day (usually the bands are similar, so don't get confused with lower bands in the morning - they could be misleading).
